subject: Public Health
title: Adjuvants: Boosting the Response
description: A micro-lesson that makes Adjuvants: Boosting the Response usable.

hook.text: Some vaccines contain aluminum salts—not as poison, but as a secret weapon that supercharges immunity.

buildup.text: Adjuvants are ingredients that enhance the immune response—making vaccines stronger with less antigen.

discovery.text: Aluminum-based adjuvants have been used safely since the 1930s—in billions of doses worldwide.

twist.text: Without adjuvants, many vaccines would be too weak to provide lasting protection—they're essential, not optional.

climax.text: New adjuvant technologies are enabling vaccines for diseases previously considered impossible to prevent.

punchline.text: The boost behind the vaccine is often the unsung hero.

quiz.question: What do adjuvants do in vaccines?
options: Enhance the immune response to make vaccines stronger
